skill.name: azure-ai-contentsafety-ts
skill.description: Analyze text and images for harmful content using Azure AI Content Safety REST SDK for TypeScript
skill.summary: Moderate user-generated content by detecting hate speech, violence, sexual content, and self-harm using Azure AI Content Safety
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced 70 false positive findings by misidentifying markdown documentation as executable code. The SKILL.md file contains TypeScript code examples using markdown code fences (```), not shell backticks. The skill legitimately requires network access to Azure Content Safety APIs and environment variables for API credentials. No malicious patterns detected after manual review.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: Example Azure endpoint URL shown in environment variable template. This is documentation showing the expected format, not an actual hardcoded credential.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URL is clearly a template example (contains <resource> placeholder) in documentation context, not actual credential leakage.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Credentials
low_findings.description: Skill accesses process.env.CONTENT_SAFETY_KEY for API authentication. This is standard and safe practice for credential management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reading API keys from environment variables is the recommended secure pattern, but should be documented as a risk factor.

content.user_title: Moderate harmful content with Azure AI Content Safety
content.value_statement: Protect your platform from harmful user-generated content including hate speech, violence, sexual content, and self-harm material. This skill integrates Azure AI Content Safety REST API to automatically analyze and flag inappropriate text and images with configurable severity thresholds.
actual_capabilities: Analyze text for hate speech, sexual content, violence, and self-harm indicators
Analyze images from base64 content or Azure blob URLs for harmful visual content
Create and manage custom blocklists for domain-specific prohibited terms
Return severity levels (0-6 or 0-7) for each detected category
Combine AI detection with custom blocklist matching for comprehensive moderation
Handle both API key and Azure AD (DefaultAzureCredential) authentication
limitations: Requires Azure Content Safety resource and valid API credentials
Network connectivity to Azure Cognitive Services endpoint required
Image analysis limited to supported formats (PNG, JPEG, etc.)
Cannot analyze video content or real-time streaming media

use_cases.title: Social Platform Content Moderation
use_cases.description: Automatically scan user posts, comments, and uploaded images before publication to detect and block harmful content that violates community guidelines.
use_cases.target_user: Community managers and social platform developers

use_cases.title: Educational Forum Safety
use_cases.description: Protect students in online learning environments by filtering hate speech, bullying content, and self-harm discussions with appropriate severity thresholds.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Review Filtering
use_cases.description: Moderate product reviews and seller communications to maintain platform quality standards and prevent abusive or inappropriate content from appearing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text for harmful content and tell me if it should be allowed: "[INSERT TEXT]"
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick moderation of a single piece of text content

prompt_templates.title: Image Content Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if this image contains harmful or inappropriate content that should be blocked from our platform: [PROVIDE IMAGE PATH OR URL]

prompt_templates.title: Custom Blocklist Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a blocklist for my gaming platform that blocks cheating-related terms, hate speech, and harassment. Add these specific terms: [LIST TERMS]
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up domain-specific content filtering rules

prompt_templates.title: Batch Content Moderation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to moderate these 50 user submissions with a maximum allowed severity of 2 (low). Flag anything above this threshold and check against my "gaming-community" blocklist: [LIST CONTENT]
prompt_templates.scenario: High-volume content moderation with custom thresholds and blocklists

output_examples.input: Analyze a comment for hate speech and violence
output_examples.output: Text flagged with severity 4 (Medium) for Hate category. Recommendation: Block or require human review before publication.
Image analysis complete. All categories at severity 0 (Safe). Content approved for publication.

output_examples.input: Moderate chat message with custom blocklist
output_examples.output: Message blocked: Contains prohibited term "cheat-code-hack" from gaming-blocklist. Severity 6 (High) for Violence category also detected.

best_practices: Always use the isUnexpected() type guard to handle API errors gracefully and prevent crashes from unexpected responses
Set category-specific severity thresholds based on your community guidelines - hate speech may need stricter limits than general violence
Maintain audit logs of all moderation decisions with timestamps, severity scores, and action taken for compliance and appeals
anti_patterns: Do not block content solely based on severity 2 (Low) - this may result in excessive false positives and user frustration
Never store API keys directly in code - always use environment variables or Azure Key Vault for credential management
Avoid making moderation decisions on empty text or corrupted images - validate input before sending to the API

faq.question: What categories of harmful content does Azure Content Safety detect?
faq.answer: Azure Content Safety detects four main categories: Hate (discriminatory language targeting identity groups), Sexual (sexual content, nudity, pornography), Violence (physical harm, weapons, terrorism), and SelfHarm (self-injury, suicide, eating disorders).

faq.question: How do severity levels work and what should I block?
faq.answer: Severity levels range from 0 (Safe) to 6 or 7 (High). Recommended actions: 0 = Allow, 2 = Review or allow with warning, 4 = Block or require human review, 6 = Block immediately. Configure thresholds based on your risk tolerance.

faq.question: Can I add custom words or phrases to block?
faq.answer: Yes. Use blocklists to define custom prohibited terms specific to your domain. Create a blocklist with the PATCH endpoint, add items with the addOrUpdateBlocklistItems action, then reference blocklistNames during text analysis.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for analysis?
faq.answer: Azure Content Safety supports common image formats including PNG, JPEG, BMP, and GIF. Images can be provided as base64-encoded content or as Azure Blob Storage URLs.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the Azure Content Safety API?
faq.answer: Two options: (1) API Key authentication using AzureKeyCredential with your CONTENT_SAFETY_KEY, or (2) Azure AD authentication using DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity or service principal scenarios.

faq.question: What happens if the API is unavailable or returns an error?
faq.answer: Always wrap API calls with isUnexpected() type guard to detect errors. On failure, decide whether to block content defensively (fail-closed) or allow it temporarily (fail-open) based on your risk tolerance. Log all errors for investigation.
